message:
feat: add ChatGPT web export import command

New CLI subcommand: cass import chatgpt <path>

Splits the monolithic conversations.json from ChatGPT web export
(Settings → Data Controls → Export) into individual conversation
files that the ChatGPT connector can index without encryption keys.

Usage:
  cass import chatgpt ~/Downloads/conversations.json
  cass import chatgpt export.json --output-dir /path/to/output
  cass import chatgpt export.json --json  # machine-readable output

Features:
- Auto-detects output directory (macOS ChatGPT app support or
  ~/.local/share/cass/chatgpt/ on Linux)
- Idempotent: skips conversations already imported
- Extracts conversation ID from "id" or "conversation_id" field
- Reports total, imported, and skipped counts

Implementation:
- Add ImportCommand enum with Chatgpt variant
- Add handle_import() dispatch function
- Add import_chatgpt_export() implementation
- Update describe_command() and is_robot_mode() for new command

Also refactored state_meta_json() to use if-let chains for cleaner
conditional DB access pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-33a7","title":"P6.14: Zero-mock enforcement + E2E logging completeness (Round 2)","description":"# P6.14: Zero-mock enforcement + E2E logging completeness (Round 2)

## Objective
Finish the last mile of the P6 no-mock test strategy by removing remaining mocks/fakes, enforcing a no-mock policy in CI, and ensuring E2E runs have complete, structured logging and unified reports.

## Background
A scan of the repo still shows explicit mock/fake usage in tests and helper code (e.g., MockDaemon in `src/search/daemon_client.rs`, fake binaries in `tests/e2e_install_easy.rs`, mock probe helpers in `src/sources/index.rs`, fake model files in `tests/semantic_integration.rs`, and synthetic "mock-*" fixtures across connector tests). This means we do not yet have full unit/integration coverage without mocks.

## Scope
- Audit remaining mocks/fakes and categorize by subsystem.
- Define a strict no-mock policy with an explicit allowlist for unavoidable OS/hardware boundaries.
- Replace mocks/fakes with real components or real fixture data.
- Unify E2E logging across Rust tests, shell scripts, and Playwright.
- Provide a single orchestration entrypoint with JSONL + Markdown reports.

## Non-Goals
- Adding new features to cass unrelated to testing.
- Rewriting existing test frameworks that already use real components.

## Required Outcomes
1. No test uses Mock*/fake/stub code paths unless explicitly allowlisted.
2. CI fails on newly-introduced mock/fake usage without a waiver.
3. E2E runs produce consistent JSONL logs and a human summary.
4. Reports capture environment metadata (OS, Rust version, git SHA, fixtures hash).

## Artifacts
- Audit report: `test-results/no_mock_audit.md` (or equivalent)
- No-mock policy doc: `TESTING.md` update
- E2E logs: `test-results/e2e/*.jsonl`
- E2E summary: `test-results/e2e/summary.md`
- Orchestration script: `scripts/tests/run_all.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tasks under this epic completed and linked.
- `rg -n "Mock|fake|stub"` in `src/` and `tests/` returns only allowlisted hits.
- `scripts/tests/run_all.sh` completes with non-zero exit on any failing suite and emits logs + summary.

## Dependencies
This epic blocks P6 (Comprehensive No-Mock Test Coverage & E2E Logging).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:00:03.817299800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T17:49:26.955858233Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T17:49:26.955793782Z","close_reason":"All P6.14 tasks complete: no-mock audit (bd-22k2), no-mock policy + CI gate (bd-28iz), daemon harness (bd-66i4), probe fixtures (bd-11is), install E2E (bd-30qg), UI fixtures (bd-1c25), semantic fixtures (bd-a63y), connector fixtures (bd-1dfc), E2E logging schema (bd-rvpc), orchestrated runner (bd-2vvo), and TESTING.md docs (bd-e0ou). CI enforces no-mock policy with 58 allowlisted patterns. Full E2E logging infrastructure with unified JSONL schema.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-11is","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:05:57.766969956Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-1c25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:06:08.553430327Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-1dfc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:08:11.229252968Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-22k2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:03:29.608020594Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-28iz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:04:25.344786197Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-2vvo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:08:36.255419656Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-30qg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:06:03.171070668Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-66i4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:05:48.369681893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-a63y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:06:15.301594645Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-e0ou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:08:41.160855115Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33a7","depends_on_id":"bd-rvpc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:08:32.018341013Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3bbv","title":"Task: Cass CLI flags + config for model/rerank/daemon","description":"## Task
Expose CLI flags and config to control model, reranker, and daemon usage.

## CLI Flags
```
--semantic
--model <winner>
--rerank
--reranker <winner>
--daemon / --no-daemon
```

## Validation & Tests
### Unit tests
- CLI parsing
- Config precedence (CLI > env > config > default)

### Integration tests
- `--help` lists eligible models
- Shell completions updated

### E2E script (detailed logging)
- `scripts/cli/cass_flags_e2e.sh`
  - Logs resolved config values

## Acceptance Criteria
- Flags documented
- Precedence verified
- Logs archived","notes":"### Optimization review
- Reuse **same precedence rules + env var names** as xf for consistency; ensure `--help` indicates baseline vs eligible models.
- E2E logs should include resolved config + eligibility status (eligible/baseline) to aid debugging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:23:21.322987036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T11:44:38.832989474Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T11:44:38.832960018Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bbv","depends_on_id":"bd-16bz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:23:21.350582199Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3tj2","title":"Wire --semantic flag to SemanticIndexer","description":"## Summary
Wire the existing --semantic flag in IndexOpts to actually trigger semantic indexing during the index command.

## Context
- GitHub Issue #41 reports --semantic flag exists but does nothing
- SemanticIndexer code exists in src/indexer/semantic.rs
- Just needs wiring into main indexer flow

## Requirements
1. When --semantic flag is passed, create SemanticIndexer
2. After text indexing, run semantic indexing on same content
3. Store vector embeddings alongside text index
4. Support incremental semantic indexing (skip already-embedded content)

## Technical Approach
In src/indexer/mod.rs, after text indexing completes:
```rust
if opts.semantic {
    let semantic = SemanticIndexer::new("fastembed", Some(&data_dir))?;
    semantic.index_messages(&messages, &mut vector_store)?;
}
```

## Files to Modify
- src/indexer/mod.rs - Wire semantic flag to SemanticIndexer
- src/lib.rs - Ensure SemanticIndexer is exported

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cass index --semantic builds vector index
- [ ] cass search --semantic uses vector similarity
- [ ] Incremental: only embeds new/changed content
- [ ] Clear error if embedding model not available","notes":"Fixed fastembed reranker init compile error (use Default for RerankInitOptionsUserDefined). Cargo test still blocked by cargo cache lock; re-run pending.

### Testing & Logging
- Unit: ensure --semantic flag toggles indexer path with correct options.
- Integration: run semantic indexing on fixture corpus and verify non-empty results.
- Logging: record index size, build time, and recall stats in JSON report.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T05:03:09.676116927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T22:02:05.513631701Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-5ix1","title":"Create test fixtures for HTML export testing","description":"## Overview
Create comprehensive test fixtures for HTML export testing, including real session data, edge cases, and malformed inputs.

## Fixture Categories

### 1. Real Session Fixtures
Located in `tests/fixtures/real_sessions/`:

```
claude_code_auth_fix.jsonl     - Multi-turn session with code, tool calls, debugging
cursor_refactoring.jsonl       - Refactoring session with large code blocks
codex_api_design.jsonl         - API design discussion with diagrams/ASCII
gemini_debugging.jsonl         - Debugging session with stack traces
```

Each fixture should include:
- 20-50 messages
- Mix of user/agent/tool/system roles
- Code blocks with various languages (rust, python, typescript, bash)
- Tool calls with inputs/outputs
- Realistic timestamps spanning a session

### 2. Edge Case Fixtures
Located in `tests/fixtures/edge_cases/`:

```rust
// empty_session.jsonl
{"agent_slug": "claude_code", "workspace": null, "messages": []}

// single_message.jsonl  
{"agent_slug": "claude_code", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]}

// 1000_messages.jsonl
// Generate programmatically with varied content

// unicode_heavy.jsonl
// Japanese, Chinese, emoji, RTL text, mathematical symbols

// all_message_types.jsonl
// One of each: user, agent, tool_call, tool_result, system
```

### 3. Malformed Fixtures
Located in `tests/fixtures/malformed/`:

```
truncated.jsonl       - Valid JSON cut off mid-stream
invalid_json.jsonl    - Syntax errors in JSON
missing_fields.jsonl  - Required fields absent
wrong_types.jsonl     - String where number expected, etc.
```

## Fixture Generation Script

```rust
// tests/fixtures/generate.rs

pub fn generate_large_session(message_count: usize) -> Conversation {
    let mut messages = Vec::with_capacity(message_count);
    let roles = [MessageRole::User, MessageRole::Agent];
    
    for i in 0..message_count {
        messages.push(Message {
            idx: i as i64,
            role: roles[i % 2].clone(),
            content: format!("Message {} with some content about topic {}", i, i % 10),
            created_at: Some(1705334400000 + (i as i64 * 60000)),
            ..Default::default()
        });
    }
    
    Conversation {
        agent_slug: "claude_code".to_string(),
        messages,
        ..Default::default()
    }
}

pub fn generate_unicode_session() -> Conversation {
    let unicode_samples = vec![
        "日本語のテキスト (Japanese text)",
        "中文文本 (Chinese text)", 
        "🎉 Emoji party 🚀💻🔥",
        "مرحبا (Arabic - RTL)",
        "∫∑∏√∞ (Mathematical)",
        "Ñoño español",
    ];
    
    // Create messages with each unicode sample
    // ...
}
```

## Code Block Fixtures

Ensure fixtures include code blocks for testing syntax highlighting:

```rust
// In fixture generation
let code_samples = vec![
    ("rust", r#"fn main() { println!("Hello"); }"#),
    ("python", "def hello():\n    print('Hello')"),
    ("typescript", "const greet = (name: string): void => console.log(name);"),
    ("bash", "#!/bin/bash\necho 'Hello World'"),
    ("json", r#"{"key": "value", "nested": {"a": 1}}"#),
    ("sql", "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 1;"),
];
```

## Tool Call Fixtures

Include realistic tool call structures:

```json
{
    "role": "tool",
    "tool_name": "read_file",
    "tool_input": {"path": "/src/main.rs"},
    "tool_output": "fn main() {\n    println!(\"Hello\");\n}",
    "status": "success"
}
```

## Fixture Validation

```rust
#[test]
fn validate_all_fixtures() {
    let fixture_dir = PathBuf::from("tests/fixtures");
    
    for entry in WalkDir::new(&fixture_dir).into_iter().filter_map(|e| e.ok()) {
        if entry.path().extension() == Some(OsStr::new("jsonl")) {
            let content = std::fs::read_to_string(entry.path()).unwrap();
            
            // Validate JSON structure
            for line in content.lines() {
                if !line.trim().is_empty() {
                    serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line)
                        .expect(&format!("Invalid JSON in {}", entry.path().display()));
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Build Integration

Add fixture generation to build.rs or test setup:

```rust
// In tests/integration/mod.rs
#[cfg(test)]
mod fixtures {
    use std::sync::Once;
    static INIT: Once = Once::new();
    
    pub fn ensure_fixtures() {
        INIT.call_once(|| {
            generate_all_fixtures();
        });
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All fixture files are valid JSONL
- [ ] Real session fixtures have 20+ messages each
- [ ] Edge case fixtures cover documented scenarios
- [ ] Malformed fixtures trigger expected errors
- [ ] Fixture generation is reproducible
- [ ] Fixtures include all 11 agent types
- [ ] Unicode fixtures render correctly
- [ ] Large session fixture (1000 msgs) exists","notes":"### Testing & Logging
- Unit: fixture loader validates JSONL per-line, counts messages/types, and asserts deterministic generator output (hash fixtures).
- Integration: run export pipeline across all fixtures and record pass/fail per fixture.
- E2E: headless browser opens a sampled export and emits JSON report with render timing + errors.
- Logging: write a fixture manifest (counts by agent/role/size) plus error log with file/line context.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T21:39:51.966647588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:45:08.454155105Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:45:08.454116312Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["html-export"],"comments":[{"id":41,"issue_id":"bd-5ix1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SCOPE CLARIFICATION: Test fixtures are for integration tests (bd-koav) and E2E browser tests (bd-2ozg) only. Unit tests (bd-2hfc, now closed) use inline fixtures and don't need external files. Priority: Generate fixtures for integration tests first.","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:46:29Z"},{"id":50,"issue_id":"bd-5ix1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Continuing work: Adding missing agent fixtures (7 remaining: aider, amp, chatgpt, cline, opencode, pi_agent, factory)","created_at":"2026-01-26T01:27:07Z"},{"id":52,"issue_id":"bd-5ix1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All acceptance criteria met:
- All 11 agent types covered: aider, amp, chatgpt, claude_code, cline, codex, cursor, factory, gemini, opencode, pi_agent
- All real session fixtures have 20+ messages (verified: 20-25 each)
- Edge case fixtures: empty_session, single_message, unicode_heavy, all_message_types, large_session (1001 msgs)
- Malformed fixtures: truncated, invalid_json, missing_fields, wrong_types
- All 97 HTML export tests pass","created_at":"2026-01-26T01:45:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-66i4","title":"P6.14c: Replace MockDaemon/MockEmbedder tests with real daemon harness","description":"# P6.14c: Replace MockDaemon/MockEmbedder tests with real daemon harness

## Goal
Eliminate `MockDaemon`, `MockEmbedder`, and `MockReranker` test doubles in `src/search/daemon_client.rs` by exercising real daemon interactions and real embedder/reranker paths.

## Why
Current unit tests rely on in-memory mock implementations, which violates the no-mock requirement and does not validate real protocol wiring, retries, or timeouts.

## Plan
1. Introduce a test-only daemon harness that starts a real daemon instance (or a real HTTP/gRPC server path) with controlled responses.
2. Use real embedder and reranker implementations (hash/fastembed) via config flags.
3. Rewrite tests in `src/search/daemon_client.rs` to call the real harness and assert on protocol outcomes (timeouts, retry-after, overload, invalid input).
4. Ensure tests remain deterministic by controlling timeouts and jitter seeds.
5. Remove or drastically reduce Mock* types in this module.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No `MockDaemon`, `MockEmbedder`, `MockReranker` types remain in `src/search/daemon_client.rs` tests unless allowlisted.
- All daemon client behaviors are validated via real transport.
- Tests are stable in CI (timeouts controlled, no flaky retries).

## Dependencies
- Requires bd-1lps (daemon client integration) to be completed.
- Uses audit results from P6.14a.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:00:46.070524885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T08:30:51.435849811Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T08:30:51.435630818Z","close_reason":"Completed ALLOWLIST documentation for MockEmbedder, MockReranker, and verified TestDaemon. Integration tests use real channel-based transport. CI no-mock gate passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66i4","depends_on_id":"bd-1lps","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:09:20.091327082Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-66i4","depends_on_id":"bd-22k2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:09:14.099717760Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8nnu","title":"Implement CDN integration with graceful fallback","description":"# Task: CDN Integration with Graceful Fallback

## Context
Enhance exported HTML with CDN-loaded libraries while ensuring the document
remains fully functional offline.

## CDN Resources

### 1. Tailwind CSS (Styling Enhancement)
```html
<!-- CDN with fallback detection -->
<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com" defer 
    onerror="document.body.classList.add('no-tailwind')"></script>
```

Inline fallback styles activated when Tailwind fails to load.

### 2. Prism.js (Syntax Highlighting)
```html
<!-- Theme -->
<link rel="stylesheet" 
    href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/prismjs@1/themes/prism-tomorrow.min.css"
    media="print" onload="this.media='all'">

<!-- Core + Autoloader -->
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/prismjs@1/prism.min.js" defer></script>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/prismjs@1/plugins/autoloader/prism-autoloader.min.js" defer></script>
```

### 3. JetBrains Mono Font (Optional Enhancement)
```html
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link rel="stylesheet" 
    href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap"
    media="print" onload="this.media='all'">
```

## Fallback Strategy

### CSS Fallbacks
```css
/* Inline critical styles that cover Tailwind absence */
.no-tailwind .container { max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 1rem; }
.no-tailwind .flex { display: flex; }
.no-tailwind .justify-between { justify-content: space-between; }
.no-tailwind .items-center { align-items: center; }
/* ... minimal utility coverage */
```

### Syntax Highlighting Fallback
```css
/* Basic code styling when Prism unavailable */
pre code:not([class*="language-"]) {
    background: var(--bg-deep);
    padding: 1rem;
    border-radius: 0.5rem;
    display: block;
    overflow-x: auto;
}
```

### Font Fallback
```css
.code-font {
    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', 
                 ui-monospace, 
                 SFMono-Regular, 
                 Menlo, 
                 Monaco, 
                 Consolas, 
                 'Liberation Mono', 
                 'Courier New', 
                 monospace;
}
```

## Offline Detection

```javascript
// Check network status and adjust UI
function checkOnlineStatus() {
    const online = navigator.onLine;
    document.body.classList.toggle('offline', !online);
    
    if (!online) {
        showToast('Offline mode: Some features may be limited', 'info');
    }
}

window.addEventListener('online', checkOnlineStatus);
window.addEventListener('offline', checkOnlineStatus);
```

## CDN Loading States

```javascript
// Track CDN resource loading
const cdnStatus = {
    tailwind: 'pending',
    prism: 'pending',
    fonts: 'pending'
};

// Update status indicators
function updateCdnStatus(resource, status) {
    cdnStatus[resource] = status;
    
    // Show indicator if any CDN failed
    const anyFailed = Object.values(cdnStatus).includes('failed');
    document.getElementById('cdn-status')?.classList.toggle('hidden', !anyFailed);
}
```

## No-CDN Mode (--no-cdns flag)

When user explicitly requests offline-only export:
```rust
pub struct HtmlExportOptions {
    pub enable_cdns: bool,  // Default: true
}

fn generate_head(options: &HtmlExportOptions) -> String {
    if options.enable_cdns {
        // Include CDN references
        include_cdn_resources()
    } else {
        // Only inline resources
        "<!-- CDNs disabled by user -->"
    }
}
```

## Subresource Integrity (SRI)

Add integrity hashes for security:
```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/prismjs@1.29.0/prism.min.js"
    integrity="sha384-xxx..."
    crossorigin="anonymous"
    defer></script>
```

## Performance Optimization

### 1. Defer Non-Critical
All CDN scripts use `defer` to not block rendering.

### 2. Media Query Trick
CSS loaded as `media="print"` then switched to `all` on load.

### 3. Preconnect
DNS lookup starts early for CDN domains.

## Testing

### Test Scenarios
1. Full connectivity: All CDNs load
2. Partial failure: Some CDNs timeout
3. Complete offline: No network at all
4. Slow connection: Resources load after content

### Automated Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_html_valid_offline() {
    let html = export_session_no_cdns(&session);
    
    // Validate HTML
    assert!(html.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"));
    
    // No external references
    assert!(!html.contains("cdn."));
    assert!(!html.contains("googleapis."));
}

#[test]
fn test_html_cdns_optional() {
    let html = export_session_with_cdns(&session);
    
    // CDNs present but with fallbacks
    assert!(html.contains("onerror="));
    assert!(html.contains(".no-tailwind"));
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTML renders completely without any CDN
- [ ] CDN resources enhance when available
- [ ] Failed CDN loads don't break the page
- [ ] No-CDN flag produces fully self-contained HTML
- [ ] SRI hashes present for security
- [ ] Loading is non-blocking","notes":"### Testing & Logging
